column1: Club Artizen Social Media Analytics Dashboard — Test Plan and Tracker

column4: Members: Kaushik, Devshree, Kushagra, Sian, Srinivas

column3: Pre-conditions
column4:
Test Description
(Steps)
column5: Expected Outcome

column3:
User is logged in as Admin.
Developer credentials for META (Facebook/Instagram) have been obtained.
column4:
1. Navigate to 'Integrations' page.
2. Click 'Connect' next to Facebook/Instagram.
3. Enter valid OAuth credentials and grant permissions.
4. Click 'Confirm'.
column5:
Facebook and Instagram channels display as 'Connected'.
System stores the access token securely.
Initial data fetch begins automatically.

column3:
User is logged in as Admin.
LinkedIn Marketing API credentials have been obtained.
column4:
1. Navigate to 'Integrations' page.
2. Click 'Connect' next to LinkedIn.
3. Enter valid API credentials.
4. Click 'Confirm'.
column5:
LinkedIn channel appears as 'Connected'.
System begins fetching LinkedIn page metrics.

column3:
User is logged in as Admin.
Google Analytics API credentials have been obtained.
column4:
1. Navigate to 'Integrations' page.
2. Click 'Connect' next to Google.
3. Enter valid OAuth credentials.
4. Click 'Confirm'.
column5:
Google channel appears as 'Connected'.
System begins fetching video Google Ads performance metrics.

column3: User is logged in as Admin.
column4:
1. Navigate to 'Integrations' page.
2. Click 'Connect' next to any channel.
3. Enter intentionally incorrect/expired credentials.
4. Click 'Confirm'.
column5:
System displays an error message prompting user to retry.
Channel does NOT appear as 'Connected'.
No token is stored.

column3:
User is logged in as Admin.
A previously connected channel has an expired OAuth token.
column4:
1. Navigate to the dashboard or Integrations page.
2. Observe the status of the channel with the expired token.
column5:
System detects the expired token and prompts the user to re-authenticate.
Channel status shows a warning/expired state until re-authentication completes.

column3:
User is logged in.
At least one social media channel is connected and has data available.
column4:
1. Navigate to the 'Statistics' page from main navigation.
2. Observe the data displayed for all connected channels.
column5:
Statistics page loads showing performance data for all connected channels.
Follower growth graph, engagement metrics, impressions/views, and CTR are all visible.

column3:
User is logged in.
At least one channel is connected with data available.
column4:
1. Navigate to 'Statistics' page.
2. Select a specific channel (e.g. Instagram) from the channel selector.
3. Observe the data.
column5:
Page updates to show individual statistics for the selected channel only.
All metrics (follower growth, engagement, impressions, CTR) are displayed for that channel.

column3:
User is logged in.
At least one channel is connected with data spanning multiple weeks.
column4:
1. Navigate to 'Statistics' page.
2. Locate the date range filter.
3. Set start date to 30 days ago and end date to today.
4. Apply the filter.
column5:
All visualisations refresh to show data within the selected 30-day range only.
Data outside the range is excluded from the charts.

column3:
User is logged in.
A channel has been connected but no data has yet been fetched.
column4:
1. Navigate to 'Statistics' page.
2. Select the newly connected channel.
column5:
System displays a loading state or a clear message indicating data is being retrieved.
No blank screen or unhandled error is shown.

column3:
User is logged in.
WhatsApp Business is connected (limited public metrics only).
column4:
1. Navigate to 'Statistics' page.
2. Select the WhatsApp Business channel.
column5:
Available metrics are displayed correctly.
Metrics unavailable via the API are clearly flagged with an explanatory note, not silently empty.

column3:
User is logged in.
At least one channel is connected with sufficient historical data.
column4:
1. Navigate to the 'Analytics' page.
2. Observe the derived insights displayed.
column5: Analytics page loads with derived insights including posting time vs. engagement correlations, top-performing post types, and location-based performance displayed as charts, tables, or ranked lists.

column3:
User is logged in.
Multiple channels are connected with data.
column4:
1. Navigate to 'Analytics' page.
2. Select 'Cross-channel' view or observe the default multi-channel display.
column5: Cross-channel insights are displayed, aggregating patterns across all connected platforms.

column3:
User is logged in.
At least one channel is connected with data.
column4:
1. Navigate to 'Analytics' page.
2. Apply a date range filter (e.g. last 90 days).
3. Apply a post type filter (e.g. Reels only).
column5:
Insights update to reflect only the filtered date range and post type.
Charts and tables re-render accordingly.

column3:
User is logged in.
A channel was very recently connected with minimal data.
column4:
1. Navigate to 'Analytics' page.
2. Select the newly connected channel.
column5:
System displays a message indicating insufficient data to generate insights.
System suggests a timeframe for when analysis will be available.

column3:
User is logged in.
At least one channel is connected with data.
Default or custom thresholds are configured.
column4:
1. Navigate to the main dashboard.
2. Observe the Health Overview Widget.
column5:
Widget shows each connected channel with a colour-coded indicator (green/amber/red).
Indicators reflect performance relative to configured thresholds.

column3:
User is logged in.
Health Overview Widget is visible with at least one channel indicator.
column4: 1. On the main dashboard, hover over or click a channel's colour-coded indicator in the Health Overview Widget.
column5: A tooltip or summary popup appears showing the specific metrics driving that channel's status (e.g. 'Engagement rate is below threshold').

column3:
User is logged in.
A previously connected channel has lost its API connection.
column4:
1. Navigate to the main dashboard.
2. Observe the Health Overview Widget.
column5:
The disconnected channel's indicator displays a grey or warning state.
A prompt is shown directing the user to reconnect the channel.

column3:
User is logged in as Admin.
No custom performance thresholds have been configured.
column4:
1. Navigate to the main dashboard.
2. Observe the Health Overview Widget.
column5:
Widget uses default benchmark values for colour-coding.
A notification informs the Admin that custom thresholds can be configured.

column3:
User is logged in.
At least one social media channel has been connected.
column4:
1. Navigate to the main dashboard.
2. Open the Widget Library panel.
3. Browse available widgets.
4. Drag a widget (e.g. 'Follower Growth') onto the dashboard canvas.
column5:
Widget appears on the canvas rendering live data.
Widget library remains accessible for placing additional widgets.

column3:
Test 19 completed.
At least one widget is placed on the dashboard.
column4:
1. Drag an existing widget to a new position.
2. Resize it by dragging its edges.
3. Save the layout.
4. Log out and log back in.
column5:
Widget moves and resizes correctly.
On re-login, the dashboard layout is restored exactly as saved.

column3:
Test 19 completed.
At least one widget is placed on the dashboard.
column4: 1. Remove a placed widget by dragging it off the canvas or clicking 'Remove'.
column5:
Widget is removed from the canvas.
Layout updates to fill the space.
Widget remains available in the Widget Library for future use.

column3:
User is logged in.
A widget exists for a channel with no data yet available.
column4:
1. Open the Widget Library.
2. Drag the widget for the data-less channel onto the canvas.
column5: Widget is placed on the canvas but displays a placeholder message indicating data is not yet available for that channel.

column3:
User is logged in with a role that permits custom widget creation.
Sufficient data exists for the selected parameters.
column4:
1. Navigate to the Widget Library or customisation panel.
2. Click 'Create Custom Widget'.
3. Select 'Engagement Rate' as primary parameter.
4. Select 'Post Type' as secondary parameter.
5. Choose 'Bar Chart' as visualisation type.
6. Name the widget 'Engagement by Post Type' and save.
column5:
A live preview of the bar chart is shown before saving.
The saved widget appears in the user's Widget Library.
Widget can be dragged onto the dashboard and displays correct data.

column3: User is logged in with widget creation permission.
column4:
1. Open the custom widget builder.
2. Select a primary parameter.
3. Select a secondary parameter incompatible with the first.
4. Attempt to choose an incompatible visualisation type.
column5:
Incompatible visualisation types are disabled/greyed out.
A tooltip explains why those options are unavailable.
User cannot save an invalid widget configuration.

column3:
User is logged in.
A channel has very limited data (fewer posts than the minimum threshold).
column4:
1. Open the custom widget builder.
2. Select parameters for the data-limited channel.
3. Observe the live preview.
column5:
System notifies the user of insufficient data to populate the widget preview.
System suggests returning once more data has been collected.

column3:
User is logged in.
Sufficient data has been collected to generate a meaningful heatmap.
column4:
1. Navigate to the Analytics page or Visualisations section.
2. Select 'Heatmap'.
3. Select 'Post Frequency vs. Click-Through Rate' as the metric.
4. Observe the rendered heatmap.
column5:
Heatmap renders correctly showing posting activity against CTR across time slots.
Hovering over cells shows specific metric values.

column3:
User is logged in.
Sufficient data has been collected.
column4:
1. Navigate to Visualisations section.
2. Select 'Spidermap'.
3. Select multi-channel performance dimensions.
4. Apply a date range filter.
column5:
Spidermap renders correctly showing multi-dimension performance across channels.
Visualisation updates to reflect the filtered date range.

column3:
User is logged in.
Only a very small number of posts exist in the selected time period.
column4:
1. Navigate to Visualisations.
2. Select 'Heatmap'.
3. Set a very narrow date range with minimal posts.
4. Attempt to generate the heatmap.
column5:
System displays a message indicating insufficient data density.
System recommends a broader date range.
No empty or misleading chart is displayed.

column3:
User is logged in.
Meta channel is connected with both paid and organic content data.
column4:
1. Navigate to the Statistics page.
2. Select the Meta/Facebook channel.
3. Locate the Paid/Organic toggle.
4. Switch to 'Paid' view.
column5:
Metrics (reach, impressions, engagement) update to show paid content data only.
Organic data is excluded from the current view.

column3:
Test 29 completed.
User is on Statistics page with Meta channel selected.
column4: 1. Switch the toggle from 'Paid' to 'Organic' view.
column5:
Metrics update to display organic content performance only.
Paid data is excluded from the view.

column3:
Test 29 completed.
User is on Statistics page with Meta channel selected.
column4:
1. Switch the toggle to 'Combined' view.
2. Apply a date range filter to a specific campaign period.
column5:
Both paid and organic metrics are shown together.
Data updates to reflect the selected campaign period.

column3:
User is logged in.
A connected channel (e.g. LinkedIn) has no paid content in the selected period.
column4:
1. Navigate to Statistics page.
2. Select the channel with no paid content.
3. Attempt to view 'Paid' data.
column5:
System displays organic data only.
A clear notification informs the user that no paid data exists for the selected channel and period.

column3:
User is logged in.
A connected channel does not support paid/organic split via its API.
column4:
1. Navigate to Statistics page.
2. Select the channel lacking API-level paid/organic split.
3. Observe the paid/organic controls.
column5:
System displays a notification explaining the API limitation for that channel.
User is not shown misleading or empty paid data.

column3:
User is logged in as Admin.
At least one social media channel is connected.
column4:
1. Navigate to the Alerts configuration section.
2. Set threshold: 'Engagement drops by 20% or more on Instagram'.
3. Save the alert configuration.
column5:
Alert threshold is saved.
System confirms that monitoring is active for the specified threshold.

column3:
Test 34 completed.
Instagram engagement drops by more than 20% (simulated or real).
column4:
1. Observe the dashboard notification centre.
2. Check the configured alert delivery method (email/message).
column5:
Alert is generated and delivered via the configured method.
Alert includes: channel name, metric, current value, threshold, and timestamp.
Alert appears in the dashboard notification centre.

column3:
Test 35 completed.
An alert is visible in the notification centre.
column4:
1. Click on the alert in the notification centre.
2. Acknowledge or dismiss the alert.
column5:
Alert is marked as acknowledged/dismissed.
Alert is retained in notification history for audit purposes.

column3:
Alert threshold is configured.
Performance metrics remain within the normal threshold range.
column4: 1. Monitor the dashboard for a period with no metric crossing the alert threshold.
column5:
No alert is generated.
System continues monitoring normally without spurious notifications.

column3:
Test 34 completed.
Alert delivery is configured via an invalid email address.
column4:
1. Trigger an alert condition (engagement drop).
2. Observe system behaviour after delivery failure.
column5:
System logs the delivery failure and retries.
Alert remains visible and accessible in the dashboard notification centre regardless of email failure.

column3:
User is logged in.
At least one channel with demographic audience data is connected.
User is on the Statistics page.
column4:
1. Open the filter panel.
2. Select Geography: 'United Kingdom'.
3. Apply the filter.
column5:
Statistics visualisations re-render showing data for the UK audience segment only.
Other geographic segments are excluded from the view.

column3:
User is logged in.
User is on the Statistics or Analytics page with a channel exposing demographics.
column4:
1. Open the filter panel.
2. Select Age: '25-34'.
3. Also select Company Size: '50-200 employees'.
4. Apply both filters simultaneously.
column5:
Data updates to reflect the combined criteria.
Only the audience matching both age and company size is shown.

column3:
Tests 39 or 40 completed.
Filters are currently active.
column4: 1. Click 'Clear all filters' on the filter panel.
column5:
All filters are removed.
Statistics and analytics views return to the full unfiltered dataset.

column3:
User is logged in.
WhatsApp Business is connected (no demographic API data).
column4:
1. Open the filter panel with WhatsApp Business selected.
2. Observe the demographic filter options.
column5:
Demographic filter options are visible but disabled for WhatsApp Business.
An explanatory message clarifies the API limitation for that channel.

column3:
User is logged in.
A channel with demographic data is connected.
column4:
1. Open the filter panel.
2. Select a very specific, niche demographic combination unlikely to match any data.
3. Apply the filter.
column5:
System displays an empty state message indicating no data matches the criteria.
System suggests broadening the filter.

column3:
User is logged in.
Sufficient historical posting and engagement data has been collected for at least one channel.
column4:
1. Navigate to the Analytics page or 'Recommendations' section.
2. Observe the optimal posting suggestions.
column5:
System surfaces recommendations per channel with optimal posting time windows and engagement uplift (e.g. 'Posts on Tuesday 18:00-20:00 receive 40% higher engagement on Instagram').
Suggestions are broken down by channel and post type.

column3:
Test 44 completed.
Posting suggestions are displayed.
column4:
1. Locate a displayed posting suggestion.
2. Click 'Mark as actioned'.
column5: Suggestion is visually marked as actioned and distinguished from pending suggestions.

column3:
User is logged in.
A channel was very recently connected with fewer posts than the minimum threshold.
column4:
1. Navigate to the Analytics or Recommendations page.
2. Select the recently connected channel.
column5:
System displays a message that recommendations are not yet available due to insufficient data.
System shows an estimated timeframe for when suggestions will appear.

column3:
User is logged in with competitor tracking permission.
A competitor's profile is publicly accessible.
column4:
1. Navigate to 'Competitor Tracking' section.
2. Enter the competitor's public social media handle or URL.
3. Click 'Track'.
column5:
System validates the profile is publicly accessible.
Competitor is added to the tracked list.
Publicly available data (follower count, posting frequency) begins populating.

column3:
Test 47 completed.
The same competitor is already in the tracked list.
column4:
1. Navigate to 'Competitor Tracking'.
2. Attempt to add the same competitor's handle again.
column5:
System alerts the user that this competitor is already being tracked.
No duplicate entry is created.

column3: User is logged in with competitor tracking permission.
column4:
1. Navigate to 'Competitor Tracking'.
2. Enter the handle of a private or inaccessible profile.
3. Click 'Track'.
column5:
System notifies the user that the profile cannot be tracked (private/inaccessible) with an explanation.
No entry is added to the tracked list.

column3:
User is logged in.
At least one competitor has been tracked (UC-12) for a sufficient period.
column4:
1. Navigate to 'Competitor Tracking'.
2. Select a tracked competitor.
3. Observe the trend analysis.
column5:
System shows content type frequency trends, posting cadence trends, and notable content strategy shifts.
Visual markers highlight significant trend changes.

column3:
User is logged in.
At least two competitors are being tracked with sufficient historical data.
column4:
1. Navigate to 'Competitor Tracking'.
2. Select multiple tracked competitors for comparison.
3. Apply a date range filter.
column5:
System renders trend comparisons across all selected competitors simultaneously.
Visualisation updates to reflect the filtered date range.

column3:
User is logged in.
A competitor was added very recently (insufficient tracking history).
column4:
1. Navigate to 'Competitor Tracking'.
2. Select the recently added competitor and view trend analysis.
column5:
System displays available limited data and indicates when fuller trend analysis will be possible.
No misleading trends are shown.

column3: A user account exists with valid login credentials.
column4:
1. Navigate to the login page.
2. Enter a valid email address and password.
3. Click 'Login'.
column5:
User is authenticated and redirected to the role-appropriate dashboard view.
Role-specific data and navigation items are loaded correctly.

column3: A user account exists.
column4:
1. Navigate to the login page.
2. Enter a valid email but an incorrect password.
3. Click 'Login'.
column5:
System displays a generic error message (not specifying which field is wrong).
User is not logged in.
User is offered the option to retry or request a password reset.

column3: A user account exists.
column4:
1. Navigate to the login page.
2. Enter incorrect credentials repeatedly, exceeding the defined failed-attempt limit.
column5:
Account is temporarily locked after the defined number of failures.
System notifies the user to contact their admin.

column3: User is logged in as Admin.
column4:
1. Navigate to 'User Management'.
2. Create a new user account.
3. Assign the 'Marketing Team' role.
4. Save.
column5:
New user account is created with Marketing Team permissions.
New user can log in and sees only Marketing Team-permitted views.

column3:
User is logged in as Admin.
A Marketing Team user account exists.
column4:
1. Navigate to 'User Management'.
2. Select the Marketing Team user.
3. Change their role to 'Co-Founder'.
4. Save.
column5:
Role is updated immediately.
On the user's next login or session refresh, they see the Co-Founder dashboard with updated permissions.

column3: A Marketing Team user is logged in.
column4: 1. Attempt to navigate directly (e.g. via URL) to a page restricted to Co-Founders or Admins only.
column5:
System redirects the user to their permitted view.
An 'Access Denied' message is displayed.
No restricted data is visible to the unauthorised user.
